issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference does not state any exam-weight percentage for Unit 7.
issues.suggested_fix: remove or rephrase to omit the percentage (e.g., 'a major unit on the AP exam'), unless an external CED citation is added to the reference doc.

issues.unsupported_claim: Peppered moths shifted 'in just fifty years' during England's Industrial Revolution
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference mentions peppered moths during the Industrial Revolution as an example of directional selection, but does not give a 50-year time span or specify England.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — drop the '50 years' and 'England' specifics, e.g., 'During the Industrial Revolution, peppered moths shifted from mostly light to mostly dark.'

issues.unsupported_claim: '…predicts antibiotic resistance, finch beaks, and even what your immune system is doing right now.'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists antibiotic resistance and Galápagos finch beaks as direct-observation evidence. The immune-system claim is not in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — drop the immune-system aside, keep antibiotic resistance and finch beaks.

issues.unsupported_claim: Specific practice-problem numbers: 400 mice, 64 white (aa), q²=0.16, q=0.4, p=0.6
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference gives the H-W equations (p+q=1, p²+2pq+q²=1) but does not provide this specific worked example.
issues.suggested_fix: acceptable as a pedagogical example since the math derives directly from cited equations; flagged only as a note. No fix needed if treated as an instructor-generated problem rather than a sourced claim.

issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Same as above — derived from cited formulas, not from the reference's content.
issues.suggested_fix: no change — derivation from H-W equations is in-bounds.

issues.unsupported_claim: Cheetah bottleneck attributed to 'after the last ice age'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference names cheetahs as a bottleneck example but does not give a cause (last ice age) or any time period.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — drop the 'after the last ice age' attribution, keep cheetahs as the bottleneck example.

issues.unsupported_claim: Amish founder example specified as 'Pennsylvania Amish'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'Amish populations, polydactyly' without naming Pennsylvania.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — say 'Amish' rather than 'Pennsylvania Amish', or add the Pennsylvania detail to the reference doc.

issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists vestigial structures as a category but does not name the human appendix.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — say 'vestigial structures' without naming the appendix, or add the appendix example to the reference doc.

issues.unsupported_claim: '…finch beaks shifting after droughts in the Galápagos.'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists 'Galápagos finch beaks' as direct-observation evidence but does not specify drought as the driver.
issues.suggested_fix: rephrase to fit reference — drop the 'after droughts' specificity, or add the drought detail to the reference doc.

issues.unsupported_claim: Specific homology examples: human arm, whale flipper, bat wing; analogy examples: bird wing vs insect wing
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference defines homologous vs analogous structures conceptually but does not name any of these specific organ examples.
issues.suggested_fix: add to reference doc — these are canonical AP examples and worth including; otherwise rephrase generically ('limb bones of different mammals', 'wings of different lineages').

issues.unsupported_claim: 'AP Biology CED Unit 7 topics 7.1–7.8' and 'College Board sample free-response questions on H-W'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference doc covers Unit 7 content but does not enumerate sub-topic numbers (7.1–7.8) or reference College Board sample FRQs.
issues.suggested_fix: remove or replace with a generic 'AP Biology Unit 7' citation, unless the CED sub-topic numbering is added to the reference doc.

overall_assessment: No fabricated names, dates, or fictional studies were detected — every flagged item is either a real-world-true elaboration (Pennsylvania Amish, human appendix, finch droughts, cheetah ice-age cause) layered on top of an example that IS in the reference, or a pedagogical derivation from a cited formula (the mouse H-W problem). The only externally-sourced numeric claims are the '13–20% of the exam' weighting and the '50 years' Industrial Revolution timeline, neither of which appears in the reference. No named individuals from M11 (Mayr, Eldredge, etc.) snuck in. Verdict is 'minor' — the script should either trim these elaborations or have them added to the reference doc, but nothing here rises to a critical hallucination.
